items.title: One of the industries that is growing rapidly due to renewable energy is HVDC- Why HVDC is Critical for Renewables: Long-Distance Transmission: Ideal ...
items.description_html:
One of the industries that is growing rapidly due to renewable energy is HVDC-

Why HVDC is Critical for Renewables:

Long-Distance Transmission: Ideal for connecting remote renewable sources (like offshore wind or desert solar) to cities with minimal power loss over long distances or undersea.

Grid Stability & Intermittency: HVDC provides superior control over power flow, allowing operators to stabilize voltage and frequency fluctuations caused by intermittent renewable sources.

Connecting Offshore Wind: HVDC is the standard for bringing offshore wind energy to shore efficiently using submarine cables.

Integrating Different Grids: It can link asynchronous power grids (running at different frequencies), which helps in trading renewable energy across regions.

Lower Transmission Costs & Infrastructure: Requires fewer conductor cables than AC, lowering material costs and reducing environmental footprint for long-distance bulk power transfer.

The Indian HVDC transmission market is experiencing rapid growth, valued at USD 3.86 billion in 2025 and projected to reach USD 5.84–6.31 billion by 2030-2031, expanding at a CAGR of 8.55%–12.6%. Driven by 500 GW renewable energy targets and long-distance power transfer needs

items.title: The Story of E 2-wheeler Sales in India - - Ola's market share has gone down to 12% from 35% in 2 years - Startup → Legacy shift is complete; Trust +...
items.description_html:
The Story of E 2-wheeler Sales in India -

- Ola's market share has gone down to 12% from 35% in 2 years
- Startup → Legacy shift is complete; Trust + distribution advantage is winning
- Ola had the first mover advantage but lost due to service backlog, perception issues, and reliability
- E 2W market expanded from 11L units to 13.5L units (FY25 to FY26)
- Ather is holding ground thanks to product development, feedback, and calculated growth

Sales Figures -

- 22% YoY growth in E-2W sales - 13.5L units sold in FY26 v/s 11.1L units in FY25
- Ola - sold only 10,118 vehicles in March 2026, a 5.4% market share. This is a sharp decline from the 22.1% share in April 2025. For FY26, Ola sold only 164,000 vehicles, less than 50% of the 344,000 units sold in FY25.
- The market is now dominated by legacy players: Bajaj Auto and TVS Motor (50% share almost)
- TVS Motor, sold 49,484 units in April 2026 (2x YoY)

no stock reco

items.title: Think RBI did what it needed to today -- not add to volatility. 🔸No jerky moves in rates or commentary. 🔸FY27 GDP growth estimate of 6.9% @$85/b...
items.description_html:
Think RBI did what it needed to today -- not add to volatility.

🔸No jerky moves in rates or commentary.

🔸FY27 GDP growth estimate of 6.9% @$85/bbl looks optimistic but may be best assessment right now.

🔸FY27 Inflation forecast at 4.6% doesn't signal an imminent rate hike. RBI Gov said as much — quite possible that rates remain low in the near to medium, he said.

🔸FX measures not permanent, RBI says.

🔸Currency intervention stance mostly unchanged. I say most because the comment on making sure "self-fulfilling expectations do not exacerbate currency movements " tells you they will give themselves a fair amount of room to act when they think they should.

Read here: https://www.reuters.com/world/india/indias-central-bank-holds-rates-iran-war-upends-economic-outlook-2026-04-08/

items.description_html:
Pre-Market Update!!

From unicorns and fibre cables to hybrid power plants and ammonia ships - India's markets are firing on all fronts.

• Swan Defence: India's First Ammonia Dual-Fuel Ships (Most Impactful)Swan Defence has secured a landmark shipbuilding order for four 92,500 DWT vessels from Energy ONE. Marking a historic breakthrough, these will be India's first ammonia dual-fuel ships, positioning the nation among global leaders in sustainable commercial shipbuilding. #swandefence

• KreditBee: Achieves Unicorn StatusFintech lender KreditBee has entered the coveted unicorn club after raising $280 million in its Series E round, achieving a valuation of $1.5 billion. It becomes FY27's first unicorn and the third Indian startup to achieve this milestone in 2026.#kreditbee

• GACM Technologies: FCCB FundraiseGACM Technologies' board has greenlit a Foreign Currency Convertible Bond issuance worth up to $699 million (~₹5,800 crore). The capital raised will support business expansion, with the company also pursuing STPI registration for software export advantages.#GACMTechnologies

• Clean Max: 185 MW Hybrid ProjectClean Max has successfully commissioned a 185 MW wind-solar hybrid facility in Gujarat. The project is anticipated to generate approximately ₹165 crore in annual revenue for FY27, catering to 15+ corporate clients under a captive power model.#CleanMax

• HFCL: Subsidiary Bags ₹1,366 Cr OFC OrderHTL Ltd, a subsidiary of HFCL, has bagged a substantial ₹1,366 crore optical fiber cable supply order from a Tier-1 domestic client. The entire supply is slated for completion by December 2026.#HFCL

• GAIL: LNG Carrier AgreementGAIL has inked a long-term charter deal with Alpha Gas for the vessel "Energy Fidelity," boasting a 174,000 cbm capacity. The pact fortifies GAIL's LNG logistics framework and ensures a stable, uninterrupted gas supply chain.#GAIL

• Prostarm: ₹165 Cr Solar EPC OrderProstarm has secured a Letter of Award from Solarium Green Energy for a 50 MWac Solar PV project in Maharashtra. The end-to-end EPC contract, valued at ₹165 crore, falls under the MAHAGENCO initiative.#Prostarm

• Infosys–Harness: AI PartnershipInfosys has joined forces with Harness to accelerate AI-driven enterprise transformation. The collaboration integrates Infosys Topaz and Cobalt with the Harness AI platform, targeting swifter software delivery, enhanced automation, and significantly reduced manual intervention.#Infosys

• Escorts Kubota: Tractor Price Hike (Least Impactful)Escorts Kubota has announced a revision in Kubota tractor prices, effective April 15, 2026. The increment differs across models, variants, and regions, reflecting updated market positioning and cost adjustments.#EscortsKubota

items.title: April is historically one of the best months of the year for stocks: Over the last 25 years, the MSCI World Index has posted positive returns 75% of t...
items.description_html:
April is historically one of the best months of the year for stocks:

Over the last 25 years, the MSCI World Index has posted positive returns 75% of the time in April.

Over this period, the average monthly return has been +2.0%, the strongest of any month.

This has been particularly driven by US stocks, which have a ~70% weight in the index.

Meanwhile, the S&P 500 has gained +1.3% on average in April since 1928, the 2nd-best month of the year after July.

This is also double the overall monthly average return of +0.7%.

Seasonality favors the bulls this month.

items.title: Trump announces a 2-week ceasefire, and most importantly, Iran has accepted it. Key Takeaways - - Negotiations are starting - The 2-week pause is subj...
items.description_html:
Trump announces a 2-week ceasefire, and most importantly, Iran has accepted it.

Key Takeaways -

- Negotiations are starting
- The 2-week pause is subject to Iran agreeing to the "complete, immediate, and safe opening" of the Strait of Hormuz
- Brent down 13%, Brent below 100$
- Iran, Oman to charge for Strait of Hormuz passage. The 2-week ceasefire plan includes allowing both Iran and Oman to charge fees on ships transiting through the Strait of Hormuz. The official said Iran would use the money it raised for reconstruction. It wasn’t immediately clear what Oman would use its money for.
- ⁠RBI monetary policy also today
- Trump says the US and Iran are "far along with a definitive agreement for long-term peace."

items.title: RT Adam Kobeissi: When we published our first playbook on January 17th, titled the "Tariff Playbook," it received a widespread positive response and 2...
items.description_html:
RT Adam Kobeissi
When we published our first playbook on January 17th, titled the "Tariff Playbook," it received a widespread positive response and 20+ million views.

But, beyond the playbook's virality, it clearly outlined our methodical approach to navigating financial markets during geopolitical conflicts under President Trump.

Today, our second playbook, the "Conflict Playbook," published on March 3rd, has progressed to Step #9 of 10, tracking just slightly behind our original 4-week timeline.

Over the coming weeks, we expect Step #10 to materialize.

items.description_html:
It's official:

Step #9 of our "Conflict Playbook" has arrived, just ~10 days after we initially expected it.

The 2-week ceasefire between the US and Iran is similar in nature to President Trump's April 2025 "tariff pause."

On April 9th, 2025, President Trump announced a "90-day" tariff pause as the bond market collapsed. Ironically, this was almost exactly one-year ago, to the day.

The 90-day pause was followed by a US-China trade deal just weeks later, with markets never retesting their lows.

Today, President Trump announced that the 2-week ceasefire between the US, Iran, and Israel will be utilized to "finalized and consummate" a lasting peace agreement between the nations.

This paves the path for Step #10 of our "Conflict Playbook" which will materialize over the coming weeks.

Pattern recognition is incredibly profitable in this market.

items.title: Iran’s strategy all along has been to impose an unbearable economic cost on the US if Trump continued the war via oil and the closure of the Strait o...
items.description_html:
Iran’s strategy all along has been to impose an unbearable economic cost on the US if Trump continued the war via oil and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

Now, oil is down ~20% well below $100 a barrel. From that perspective, it’s clear who benefits most from the 2-week ceasefire off ramp. That’s Trump.

But the question is: would Tehran extract significant concessions in the negotiations in terms of sanctions relief and/or somehow Hormuz control? Perhaps that’s the quid pro quo.

items.title: BREAKING: Iran says it has "forced" the US to accept its "10-point plan" which includes the following terms: 1. Commitment to non-aggression 2. Iran’...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: Iran says it has "forced" the US to accept its "10-point plan" which includes the following terms:

1. Commitment to non-aggression
2. Iran’s control over the Strait of Hormuz
3. Acceptance of Iran's uranium enrichment
4. Lifting of all primary sanctions
5. Lifting of all secondary sanctions
6. Termination of all UN Security Council resolutions
7. Termination of all Board of Governors resolutions
8. Paying compensation to Iran
9. Withdrawal of US combat forces from the region
10. Cessation of war on all fronts, including in Lebanon

Trump says this plan is "a workable basis."

items.title: BREAKING: Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi releases a statement responding to President Trump's two-week ceasefire announcement. Full statement below:...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi releases a statement responding to President Trump's two-week ceasefire announcement.

Full statement below:

"On behalf of the Islamic Republic of Iran, I express gratitude and appreciation for my dear brothers HE Prime Minister of Pakistan Sharif and HE Field Marshal Munir for their tireless efforts to end the war in the region.

In response to the brotherly request of PM Sharif in his tweet, and considering the request by the U.S. for negotiations based on its 15-point proposal as well as announcement by POTUS about acceptance of the general framework of Iran’s 10-point proposal as a basis for negotiations, I hereby declare on behalf of Iran’s Supreme National Security Council:

If attacks against Iran are halted, our Powerful Armed Forces will cease their defensive operations.

For a period of two weeks, safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz will be possible via coordination with Iran’s Armed Forces and with due consideration of technical limitations."

items.title: Wealth destruction in the last 5 years from what was once considered a wealth creating defensive sector - City Gas Distribution space (IGL, Gujarat Ga...
items.description_html:
Wealth destruction in the last 5 years from what was once considered a wealth creating defensive sector - City Gas Distribution space (IGL, Gujarat Gas, MGL)

Key reasons why this happened⏬

>> Reduction in cheaper APM gas allocation

- Earlier CGD companies used to receive higher allocation of low cost domestic APM gas which supported strong EBITDA margins
- Over the last few years this allocation was reduced gradually
- Companies had to shift towards higher cost gas sourcing impacting profitability visibility

>> Increasing regulatory & policy intervention

- Sector moved from predictable margin visibility to policy dependent earnings visibility
- Government priority remained consumer affordability especially in CNG pricing

This led to pressure on EBITDA per SCM across the sector since rising input gas costs were not fully passed on to customers

Earlier CGD companies traded at premium multiples due to predictable cash flow visibility but not any more

Also Change in monopoly perception after new CGD bidding rounds & threat of EV made the sector less attractive

Most of these co's have trading at 10x on Cashflow basis with cash rich b/s (this happened after the severe de-rating)

Markets now treating them like value stocks.

items.title: This is Bikaner in Rajasthan and it is April. Nothing here is supposed to be white. But during April 2-4, 2026, the sky unleashed repeated spells of r...
items.description_html:
This is Bikaner in Rajasthan and it is April. Nothing here is supposed to be white. But during April 2-4, 2026, the sky unleashed repeated spells of rain and sudden barrages of hail, with some hailstones as large as lemons. “One hundred per cent gone,” said Bhagirath Karwasra, a farmer in the district, when asked about crop damage. Wheat, chana and mustard growing in his field have all been wiped out.

@shagun__k reports that the heavy losses are widespread across Rajasthan and Punjab. The hailstroms and gusty winds during this time of the year fall outside that risk calendar of north India's farmers, who are now dealing with a new period of crop loss.

It is the kind of loss that crop insurance is supposed to soften. But delayed insurance payments have heightened the distress. Read the full story: https://www.downtoearth.org.in/agriculture/caught-off-guard-unseasonal-hailstorms-rain-wipe-out-entire-fields-in-rajasthan-punjab-days-before-harvest

Photos: Bhagirath Karwasra

items.description_html:
The US and Israel maintained attacks on Iran hours before President Donald Trump’s deadline for Tehran to agree to a ceasefire plan, clouding prospects of an imminent resolution to the war.

Iran’s semi-official Mehr news agency said explosions were heard on Kharg Island, the country’s main oil-export hub. Fox News said the US targeted bunkers, a radar station and ammunition storage there, alongside an unintentional hit on the island’s landing docks.

Iran kept up attacks across the Persian Gulf on Tuesday, with Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates both reporting missile fire.

https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-07/iran-keeps-up-attacks-before-us-deadline-dimming-peace-chances?srnd=homepage-asia

items.title: This is why almost every IPO-bound AI firm is hyping the story. AI only regurgitates public, easily available info. There is barely any second-level t...
items.description_html:
This is why almost every IPO-bound AI firm is hyping the story. AI only regurgitates public, easily available info. There is barely any second-level thinking (Howard Marks), yet HNIs and institutions are letting AI’s output steer their decisions. On social media, armies of agents peddle snake oil, convincing you how “strong” AI is and what you’re “missing” by ignoring it.

Deep thinking cannot be outsourced. Nah!!

And this, ironically, is what a very popular AI engine just told me. 🙂

"Neuland's CMS work is characterised by genuine technical complexity — high-potency APIs, difficult chiral chemistry, peptide synthesis — which creates deeper process intimacy and higher switching costs than Laurus's synthesis division, which is still building its CDMO credentials and track record. Laurus's core identity remains an ARV API manufacturer; the CDMO ambition is real but the depth of customer embedding is lower."

items.title: Was reading the strategy note of MOSL - Good data - After a recent correction of ~10% since the start of the Iran-Israel/US war, valuations have becom...
items.description_html:
Was reading the strategy note of MOSL -

Good data - After a recent correction of ~10% since the start of the Iran-Israel/US war, valuations have become much more sober, with the Nifty trading at 18x (a 14% discount to the LPA of 20.9x). The Indian market has dipped 3%, while the MSCI EM has risen 31%, resulting in an underperformance of 34% in FY26 – likely the most severe over the past two decades. The Indian market’s valuation premium vs. EM has shrunk to 27% vs. a 10-year avg. of 73%, a peak of 145%, and close to a multi-decadal low of 21%.

India now appears better poised within the EM equity space

Valuations, shorts in the system, pessimistic view in the market - all of this together can gear us up for a big rise if the war cools down and crude cools down

items.title: Zepto has received in-principle approval from the market regulator SEBI for its IPO and is targeting a listing as early as June this year. This is imp...
items.description_html:
Zepto has received in-principle approval from the market regulator SEBI for its IPO and is targeting a listing as early as June this year.

This is important as it comes at a time when market volatility has pushed PhonePe to delay its IPO, despite reducing its valuation expectation by over 30% from $15 billion to $10 billion.

However, PhonePe can hold back its IPO because its entire issue was an offer for sale, and it can always rely on its parent company, Walmart, for additional funding.

But Zepto needs primary capital.

That’s because it has about $600–700 million in cash in the bank as of March 2026, while both key rivals, Blinkit (Eternal) and Swiggy, are sitting on close to a $2 billion war chest.

Like Swiggy, Zepto’s burn rate remains elevated as it focuses on higher-order volumes as compared to AOV growth chased by other rivals.

So, Zepto needs fresh capital — likely through an IPO or a private funding round — but valuation will come under pressure in both cases.

Its last private valuation was $7 billion. The price discovery process is underway — the next few weeks will be interesting.

https://www.thearcweb.com/article/zepto-sebi-ipo-aadit-palicha-quick-commerce-CLqE7WXLua4zj0iR

items.title: RT Internet Freedom Foundation (IFF): IFF Statement on Today's MeitY In-Person Meeting on the Proposed Amendments to the IT Rules, 2021 IFF's Founder ...
items.description_html:
RT Internet Freedom Foundation (IFF)
IFF Statement on Today's MeitY In-Person Meeting on the Proposed Amendments to the IT Rules, 2021

IFF's Founder Director, Apar Gupta, attended a consultation meeting convened today at the MeitY office on the proposed amendments to the Information Technology (Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code) Rules, 2021. We share the following account in the interest of transparency.

On the structure of the meeting
We noticed that a separate meeting had been convened earlier for industry stakeholders. However, even then private sector representatives were present in the session we attended as well. Civil society participation was sparse, with only three to four organisations in the room. This speaks to the gradual erosion of capacity and shrinking number of civil society organisations in India that work on digital rights.

We thanked MeitY for holding the meeting and encouraged it to consistently invite civil society organisations and independent experts to similar dialogues. Sustained engagement of this nature is essential to deepen institutional trust and improve policy foresight. At the same time, we placed on record our concern regarding the continuing lack of adherence to the Pre-Legislative Consultation Policy, 2014, and the urgent need for greater transparency in the processes through which policy and regulation are framed.

On the substance of the discussion
At the commencement of the meeting, the MeitY Secretary, who chaired the proceedings, indicated that certain modifications to the draft amendments are under consideration in light of feedback received from industry. These reportedly include the creation of a master index of all advisories and clarification, with an indication of whether each is mandatory in nature; and the introduction of greater definitional clarity around terms such as "news" and "current affairs", which the proposed amendments seek to extend in their application to social media users.

Our submission
Our oral intervention restated the position set out in IFF's formal written comments that the proposed amendments to the IT Rules, 2021 must be withdrawn in their entirety. We also stated more than 600 individuals (750+ now, yay!) have submitted their responses to MeitY using a public template made available by IFF.

We submitted, in brief, that the modifications indicated at the meeting do not fundamentally alter the illegality or the censorial impact of the proposed changes. We grounded this position in the framework of the Information Technology Act, 2000, and in the existing patterns of opaque censorship under the Rules that operate without any meaningful adherence to the principles of natural justice. We concluded by reiterating our demand for a complete withdrawal of the proposed amendments and for substantially greater transparency in the rule-making process going forward.

Apar had to leave the meeting before its conclusion and provided a note of regret to MEITY.

A note of thanks, and a continuing call to action

IFF is grateful to every Indian who has spoken out against these proposed changes and participated in the consultation process. We would underline that today's meeting, limited as the civil society presence was, has come about only because of sustained public backlash. That pressure matters. It is what creates space for civil society at tables from which it would otherwise be excluded.

We urge you to continue writing to MeitY. Please continue to speak up. Your voice matters.

Our submission and how you can send MEITY an email : https://internetfreedom.in/iffs-comments-on-the-meitys-proposed-amendments-to-the-2021-it-rules/


Internet Freedom Foundation (IFF): IFF will be attending an in-person consultation with MeitY today at 1 PM in Delhi on the draft amendments to the IT Rules, 2021. We will place on record our concerns on their impact on free speech, due process and platform accountability. Since this consultation is being held

items.title: Have been reading about this for a while now. India has a specifically important need for Fast Breeders. The control of uranium is with other countrie...
items.description_html:
Have been reading about this for a while now. India has a specifically important need for Fast Breeders. The control of uranium is with other countries and India has too little - and what it has, the real fissile stuff (U235) is way too little. We get more U238 - an isotope that isn't fissile by itself so in an FBR, it's combined with plutonium (Pu239) which produces energy + more Pu 239.

The energy is good for usage. The Pu produced is put back in as input - and when enough's there, you can use Thorium alongside and produce U-233. The idea of criticality is when the reaction is stable and producing enough neutrons to sustain fission but not too much or too fast.

The idea then is we can use U238, which is relatively more abundant, to produce plutonium (input fuel for FBRs) and also to produce U233 which is useful along with Thorium in the next stage of reactors which are more efficient energy producers.

For india, this means we can use our thorium reserves with relatively less uranium, and the more abundant form of uranium, to get more nuclear energy. India has among the highest thorium reserves in the world but very little uranium, and having other countries as a source of uranium is a bad idea (as we can see, every one is for themselves these days)

It will take another 20+ years to get there. But remember, this plan was set up in 1954, so it's been a long time already. It will be useful for India to get way more energy without having to rely on external sources, and the nuclear path is a good way ahead. The FBR breakthrough was needed, now I think we might need to wait for 5+ years to get enough U 233 that will help in the next stage. Nothing's sudden until it actually happens.

CreditAccess Grameen : Business Update

Must Read if you track the MFI sector

FY26
GLP⏫14% - 29,590 Cr
Disbursement⏫24% - 24,860

Q4 Disb ⏫28% - 8,313 Cr (QoQ⏫44%)🔥

Portfolio stress indicators improved meaningfully
- PAR 0+ declining to 3.0% from 4.4%
- PAR 30+ reducing to 2.7% from 3.8%
- PAR 90+ improving to 2.3% from 2.9%

PAR 1-90 has returned to pre-crisis levels & X bucket collection efficiency reached 99.84%🟩

Normalisation in asset quality visible across geographies

Monthly PAR 15+ accretion continuously trending lower (means that fewer loans are newly slipping into the 15+ days overdue category each month)

Another structural shift is the sharp increase in the share of retail finance within the portfolio. Retail loans now contribute 18% of the book compared to just 6% a year earlier. Lenders are actively reducing the dependence on the traditional group lending model.

Overall, CreditAccess Grameen is entering FY27 with improving asset quality, strong disbursement momentum, rising retail portfolio contribution & better geographic diversification.

items.title: Kalyan, Senco and PNGS Reva all dropped Q4 updates and the headlines look epic but there is one thing interesting here - Kalyan India +65%, Senco +46%...
items.description_html:
Kalyan, Senco and PNGS Reva all dropped Q4 updates and the headlines look epic but there is one thing interesting here - Kalyan India +65%, Senco +46%, PNGS Reva +139%

Here's the problem -

Senco in their press release mentioned average realisation went from ₹84,782/10g in Q4FY25 to ₹1,51,783/10g in Q4FY26, +79% YoY, with another +20% on top QoQ. Assuming that should be true approximately for all the other players and run that math against the SSSG prints which inverts the picture. Senco's 34% SSSG on 79% gold means same-store gram volumes are down ~25%. Kalyan's vaunted 45% India SSSG? Volumes down ~19%.

Most of what you see is value gains rather than volume gains

Seeing this Senco launched 9k gold collection called Cloud 9 - 37.5% purity vs 22k's 91.6%, a roughly 60% cut in gold content per piece.

You don't engineer a structurally cheaper SKU in the same quarter you're reporting +46% growth unless you've privately concluded the consumer can't follow gold any higher. Kalyan is making the same bet through a different door they opened 14 Candere stores vs 28 Kalyan stores in Q4, and Candere (digital-first, diamond-led, lightweight) is now 124 stores against Kalyan India's 342, ~27% of the network. Candere did +360% in Q4 off a small base. Both companies are quietly hedging the 22k wedding-store format with affordability formats

Senco's FY27 guidance closes the loop. They've guided 20–25% value growth after delivering ~35% in FY26, with EBITDA margin held flat at 7.5–7.8% as operating leverage would be difficult with just value growth in place. Meanwhile Kalyan's ME segment grew 39% (volume down ~22% by the same gold math) with Israel-Iran spillover hitting walk-ins for three of four weeks in March and building material disruption delaying new showroom launches; international is 11% of revenue but a higher share of profit but if the issue is elongated than it can be a pain on international EBITDA. The Akshaya Tritiya tell is that both Kalyan and Senco called advance bookings "encouraging"

Disclaimer - Not a recommendation to buy/sell

items.title: On a humid morning in Fukuoka, a coastal city in southern Japan, a new kind of power came online. Japan has launched Asia’s first osmotic power plant...
items.description_html:
On a humid morning in Fukuoka, a coastal city in southern Japan, a new kind of power came online. Japan has launched Asia’s first osmotic power plant, which generates electricity by mixing fresh water with salt water.

“It’s a meaningful plan—the start of a plan, perhaps—in our response against climate change,” said Kenji Hirokawa, director of the Seawater Desalination Center, which runs the facility, as per Gizmodo.

Fukuoka’s plant is only the second of its kind worldwide, following one in Denmark that opened in 2023. Japan’s version is larger and marks a step forward for this little-used but promising renewable energy source.

The plant will generate about 880,000 kilowatt hours of electricity per year—enough to help run a nearby desalination facility and supply around 220 homes. That equals the output of two soccer fields of solar panels, but osmotic power keeps running day and night, in any weather

items.title: ET Article - Mangalore, Bhopal & Aurangabad Step Up to Answer the Call of GCCs; Tier-2 cities now make up 30–32% of all new job postings, despite slo...
items.description_html:
ET Article -

Mangalore, Bhopal & Aurangabad Step Up to Answer the Call of GCCs; Tier-2 cities now make up 30–32% of all new job postings, despite slower growth

India’s global capability centres (GCCs) are expanding beyond traditional hubs, with the first set of Tier-2 cities such as Mangalore, Bhopal, and Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar emerging as key locations for new tech hubs, driven by cost advantage and access to talent

While GCCs' move into smaller cities such as Coimbatore, Indore, and Lucknow is well documented, more centres are coming up as states push policies and build infrastructure to attract such investments

Tier-2 cities now make up 30–32% of all new job postings, according to data.

Hiring demand for tech and GCC roles in cities like Mangalore and Indore has picked up notably, driven by the rise of nano-GCCs. Demand for such locations has risen to ~35% of new evaluations in FY25, up from 14.9% in FY24

Key Drivers

Cost arbitrage vs Tier-1 cities
Access to untapped talent pools
State-level incentives & infrastructure push
AI-led distributed work models

no reco

items.title: Nearly 95% of Indian households have access to basic drinking water, a rate of progress that has surpassed the world's average. Tap or piped water is ...
items.description_html:
Nearly 95% of Indian households have access to basic drinking water, a rate of progress that has surpassed the world's average. Tap or piped water is the single largest source of drinking water for urban Indians.

The most recent data on access to drinking water in India comes from the 79th round of the National Sample Survey (NSS), conducted in 2022-23.

Nearly seven in ten households across urban India use tap water as their principal source of drinking water. The principal source refers to the one from which most of the drinking water was obtained by the household during the last 365 days.

However, there are some key peculiarities at the state level. Bottled water (which includes home-delivered cans of water) has become an important source of drinking water in the southern states.

In urban Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu and Telangana, over a quarter of households get their drinking water primarily from bottled water. In Kerala, on the other hand, household wells are the most important source of drinking water.

Driven by the southern states, bottled water has become a key source of drinking water in urban India, now serving 15% of households nationally.

How does bottled water use compare in rural India? Read @Rukmini's analysis to find out: https://www.dataforindia.com/access-to-drinking-water/?utm_source=twitter&utm_medium=post

#Water #WASH #WashData #India #DataForIndia

items.description_html:
About 60–70% of Amazon’s quick commerce customers in India are existing users (including Amazon Fresh), while the rest are coming from other quick commerce apps.

UBS says horizontal commerce platforms such as Amazon and Flipkart “have cannibalization of 20-30% of their existing business.”

Right now, quick commerce units at Amazon and Flipkart are primarily focused on grocery/FMCG SKUs, and cannibalization will increase as they expand into core e-commerce categories.


Madhav Chanchani: Amazon India’s quick commerce unit is doing 300-400k orders per day, according to UBS estimates.

How does that compare with others?

Blinkit: 2.6 mn orders per day

Swiggy Instamart: 1.2 mn orders per day

Reliance’s JioMart: 1.6 mn orders per day (as of December)

items.title: RT Vasant Shetty | Building Mundhe Banni: Our next story is about the Rocky Bhai of the seafood business. A boy from Mysore, with absolutely no backgr...
items.description_html:
RT Vasant Shetty | Building Mundhe Banni
Our next story is about the Rocky Bhai of the seafood business.

A boy from Mysore, with absolutely no background in seafood, is today running a global seafood company. From the Atlantic to the Pacific, from the Arabian Sea to the Indian Ocean, his company operates across oceans, runs frozen food processing factories in multiple European countries, has offices in more than 10 countries, and sells fish across the world, generating Rs 10,000 crore in revenue.

And here is the surprising part. He comes from a very humble, middle class background in Mysore. You cannot get a more rooted Mysorean Kannadiga than @ksutham.

What he has built is a truly fascinating story. He is also an incredible storyteller. We often hear that Kannadigas are less ambitious, that they are content with what they have. This story is 180 degrees opposite to that belief. Here is someone with the ambition to conquer the world and build the largest seafood company on the planet.

This is a story for anyone who feels limited by where they come from. You did not go to IIT. You did not have wealthy parents. You did not have constant guidance. You did not have generational wealth. You did not come from a business background. None of that has to be an impediment.

If you have the clarity to know what you want, the willingness to figure things out, and the mindset to ask what is the worst that can happen, you can build something extraordinary.

This is a story we truly, truly enjoyed producing. The trailer drops today, and the episode goes live this Friday, the 10th.

@Shishir_S_U @mundhebanni


Vasant Shetty | Building Mundhe Banni: With the new clue, many got it right..🙌

Our next guest is @ksutham, founder of Captain Fresh. He is literally the Rocky Bhai of sea food business.

Absolutely roller coaster ride...

Trailer loading.. Episode on 10th April..

items.title: P/B ratio has historically been a more reliable and stable valuation metric for predicting future returns compared to alternatives such as P/E or EV/E...
items.description_html:
P/B ratio has historically been a more reliable and stable valuation metric for predicting future returns compared to alternatives such as P/E or EV/EBITDA. I had demonstrated this through correlation analysis in an earlier investor memo.

The chart below tracks the P/B ratio (blue line) of the Nifty Smallcap 100 Index against the 25th percentile of its trailing 5-year P/B range (orange line). In simple terms, when the blue line falls below the orange line, the current valuation is in the lowest quartile of the past five years.

Since P/B is broadly proportional to ROE, the structural improvement in index ROE—from ~8–12% in the previous decade to ~15% currently—has led to a gradual upward shift in the orange line over time.

Notably, for the first time since the COVID crash, the current P/B has moved below this 25th percentile threshold. Historically, similar instances—during the 2011–2013 downturn, Q1 2016 correction, and the COVID crash—have been followed by strong returns over the subsequent 1–3 years.

This does not imply that markets have bottomed, nor does it guarantee short-term gains. In an event-driven environment, especially with geopolitical risks such as an escalation in war, markets could decline further by 10–20%.

However, what can be said with a reasonably high degree of confidence is that forward-looking returns over a 1–3 year horizon from such valuation levels have historically been better than average.

items.title: There are frequent debates in India about what exactly forced the British to grant us Independence in 1947. It was a combination of factors that inclu...
items.description_html:
There are frequent debates in India about what exactly forced the British to grant us Independence in 1947. It was a combination of factors that included a war-weary Britain, and the long-running political mobilisation in India. However, the fear of guerrilla warfare by the revolutionaries, and the possibility of a revolt in the Indian armed forces (esp after INA & Naval revolt) played a major role. We do not need to debate this because Prime Minister Attlee has explicitly noted this in the Transfer of Power papers (Nov 1946). Just look up Vol 9, doc 35, page 68 (199 in the pdf):

https://apnaorg.com/wp/books/transfer-of-power-in-india-1942-to-1947-vol-9/

items.title: Mr Ramesh Damani on how his father was one of the Earliest Value investors - “I was born in the city of Hyderabad, but fairly early on in my life, my...
items.description_html:
Mr Ramesh Damani on how his father was one of the Earliest Value investors -

“I was born in the city of Hyderabad, but fairly early on in my life, my parents moved to Mumbai. Even in those days, which were the early 1960s, some of my fondest memories were of my father's involvement in the stock exchange

The residence where I live today, and even then, is just across the stock exchange. My father, Mr. Shrichand Damani, started in the stock market in the 1960s, when it was very small. There was an influx of stock market people then. A lot of old stock market hands lived in my building.

I remember when I went to school, my father was the only one in the stock market. Most of the students had their fathers working in MNCs or running their own business. My father’s profession puzzled me a lot. What was it that my father did? Then the market was extremely small, so there wasn’t much money to be made.

Early years were a struggle for the family. But within that subset of investors - and I know because I met his friends later - my dad was considered a better-informed investor of his era. This was primarily because he was a voracious reader, a habit that he passed on to me. He inculcated the habit of reading the international press very early in me.

So at a time when companies like Nestle, Siemens, etc. were coming to India, he was already familiar with them. That gave him a leg up over his contemporaries, who only knew Tata Steel or Tata Motors, and didn’t know what MNCs or globalization would mean.

I remember him speaking very fondly in the 1960s about companies like Specialty Foods (which later became Nestle) and ABB. So he was amongst the early value investors who bought MNC Stocks”

From the Book Masterclass with Super Investors; no stock reco

items.title: RT cecfee: Heat’s impact on students is well documented. But what about early childhood, when cognitive foundations are formed? A new study by Bensto...
items.description_html:
RT cecfee
Heat’s impact on students is well documented. But what about early childhood, when cognitive foundations are formed?

A new study by Benston John, E. Somanathan and Rohini Somanathan shows that a simple intervention -painting anganwadi roofs white - reduced indoor heat and improved children’s cognitive performance by 6–7%.

In a new article of @Ideas4India, the authors argue that findings from this study are striking, not just because of the learning gains, but because it comes from a low-cost intervention (around ₹1 per child per day) that requires no ongoing energy use.

Read the article: https://www.ideasforindia.in/topics/human-development/cooling-classrooms-to-protect-human-capital-evidence-from-keralas-anganwadis

Link to the study: https://www.isid.ac.in/~cecfee/publication/when-the-roof-reflects-heat-learning-and-adaptation-in-early-childhood-settings/

@emc_kerala | Environment for Development

items.description_html:
FIIs sold ₹1.6 lakh crores in 2025 and ₹1.1 lakh crores in just the first 3 months of 2026

Rs 2.7 Lakh cr of investments exited India in just 15 months

If SIP Book hadn't supported the DIIs, our markets would have been under the ground

Requesting the Government to reward equity investors through -

1 - LTCG/STCG tax reduction + Capital Gains Tax Stability Framework - Commit to a 5–10 year tax roadmap for equities (no surprises).
2 - Conversation with FIIs to bring them back (cut down on the operational hassles and taxation issues)
3 - Introduce Tax deductions for equity SIPs (like 80C-lite category)
4- Position India as “China+1 capital market” or play on the Geopolitical neutral image
5 - Push settlement of trades/investments in INR globally - Reduces FX risk dependency
6 - Create a Sovereign Wealth fund via LIC and other big players in the war Footing
7 - Build more on Mutual Fund Sahi hain Campaign - More Campaigns can be created, such as Equity investments Sahi hain, har mahine Stock Investment Yojana, and so on

Also, we need to stabilize our currency, move away from massive imports in Oil, Fertilizers, and Semiconductors (thanks to EVs/CNG and other platforms in Mobility and Grid movement towards renewables, we are taking the right steps)

Fingers crossed!

items.title: RT Aakash Gupta: This is wild: Citrini sent a dude with $15,000 cash, recording sunglasses, and a pack of Cuban cigars to the Strait of Hormuz. What h...
items.description_html:
RT Aakash Gupta
This is wild: Citrini sent a dude with $15,000 cash, recording sunglasses, and a pack of Cuban cigars to the Strait of Hormuz. What he found flips everything Wall Street thinks about the strait on its head.

Every hedge fund, every macro desk, every retired general on CNBC is watching the same AIS shipping data to price Hormuz risk. The analyst signed a pledge at an Omani checkpoint promising not to gather information, then smuggled in a gimbal, a microphone kit, and a 150x zoom Leica camera past the border officer who inspected his bag.

What he discovered on the ground: the AIS data everyone is trading on is missing roughly half of what's actually transiting the strait on any given day. Ships are going dark, spoofing destinations, broadcasting "CHINESE CREW OWNER" through transponder fields to avoid getting hit. Iran's ghost fleet is running 29+ laden tankers inside the Gulf with transponders off, moving an estimated $3B in crude to Malaysia since the war started.

The entire market is pricing a "closed" strait off satellite imagery and transponder data that has a 50% blind spot. Every oil model, every supply forecast, every macro call built on AIS throughput numbers is working from a dataset that systematically overstates the disruption.

When the signals deliberately go dark, the people staring at dashboards are the last to know what's happening. Citrini figured that out by putting a guy on a speedboat 18 miles from the Iranian coast while Shahed drones flew overhead.

The gap between "what AIS says" and "what's actually transiting" is the most mispriced variable in energy right now.


Citrini: Strait of Hormuz: A CitriniResearch Field Trip

The Field Report from Analyst #3 is live.

https://www.citriniresearch.com/p/strait-of-hormuz-a-citrini-field

items.title: Looks like a distress sale to Nykaa, given that Deepika Padukone’s 82°E saw revenues fall by around 30% to Rs 15 crore in FY25 and continues to be l...
items.description_html:
Looks like a distress sale to Nykaa, given that Deepika Padukone’s 82°E saw revenues fall by around 30% to Rs 15 crore in FY25 and continues to be loss-making.

82°E’s last valuation was Rs 90 crore, with Padukone and Ranveer Singh holding about a 15% stake, according to Tracxn.

Co-founder Jigar Shah, who holds 56%, left the full-time role at 82°E to pursue an investment banking gig last year.

Nykaa has seen success with acquisitions like Dot & Key, which it acquired in 2021. Its revenues have grown over 20x since then, from roughly Rs 27 crore in FY21 to over Rs 529 crore by FY25.

Nykaa also has experience with celebrity brands through Kay Beauty, which it co-founded with Katrina Kaif - the brand recorded a 50% revenue jump to Rs 132.4 crore in FY25.

It will be interesting to see what Nykaa can do with 82°E if the deal goes through.

items.title: Dmart's Q4FY26 update shows that it has reached 500 stores DMart opened its very first store in 2002 in Powai, Mumbai 24 years to reach 500 stores! Ju...
items.description_html:
Dmart's Q4FY26 update shows that it has reached 500 stores

DMart opened its very first store in 2002 in Powai, Mumbai

24 years to reach 500 stores! Just 20 stores per year and around 2 stores per month/1 store per 14 days

Dmart has always been cautious about opening new stores - Slow and steady does win the race!

But make no mistake, their stores are Huge - DMart stores typically average around 30,000–40,000 sq. ft., with a recent shift towards larger "hypermarket-style" stores often reaching 50,000–60,000 sq. ft and these are mostly owned properties

We all know about the other retailer that went berserk on store additions and eventually went bankrupt due to an overload of debt

no stock reco

items.title: RT NIK: 🚨OpenAI CFO Says Company Not Ready For IPO 2026, Altman Stopped Inviting Her To Financial Meetings with Investors >be sam altman >commit to...
items.description_html:
RT NIK
🚨OpenAI CFO Says Company Not Ready For IPO 2026, Altman Stopped Inviting Her To Financial Meetings with Investors

>be sam altman
>commit to $600 billion in cloud spending over 5 years
>will burn $200B+ before generating cash revenue
>but revenue growth already slowing
>cfo tells colleagues she has concerns
>says company won't be ready for 2026 ipo
>your move?
>stop inviting her to financial meetings
>change her reporting structure so she no longer reports to you
>problem solved

CFO Sarah Friar has been telling colleagues OAI won't be ready for 2026 IPO as revenue growth will not support the spending.

CEO Sam Altman wants to IPO before Anthropic does. So, he excluded her from compute spending meetings with top investors.

Investors said her absence was "noticeable and awkward."

OpenAI tried to own data centers via Stargate $500B joint venture with Oracle and SoftBank. Lenders said no, wouldn't back "a company with an unproven business model burning billions annually." So now they are forced to rent instead.

Remember the $122B they just raised? They actually only got $25B; the rest is conditioned to "if you IPO"

Dario Amodei: "I kind of get the impression that some of the other companies have not written down the spreadsheet, that they don't really understand the risk they're taking."

OpenAI might not actually make it to IPO...

items.title: Ramesh Damani Sir’s first success story – "In 1993,I decided to take a crack at investing. Luckily for me, Infosys had gone public in 1993, and I ha...
items.description_html:
Ramesh Damani Sir’s first success story –

"In 1993,I decided to take a crack at investing. Luckily for me, Infosys had gone public in 1993, and I had a technology background. So when I heard about the company, I immediately understood the value proposition.

I knew that in programming, one would charge USD 100 in the US for the work being done in India at USD 10. Preliminary checks showed good corporate governance and a very professional team.

A lot of the old hands in Dalal Street didn’t understand technology since there were no physical assets, and all the real assets walked out at five in the evening. They had no idea how to value these companies. But I understood it since I had worked in that industry.

Steve Jobs talks about connecting the dots, and that’s how my investing life connected with my technology background. So we were amongst the first wave of investors that bought Infosys in 1992-93. By the time we sold it in 2000, the stock must have gone up 500x. It was a lucky first strike - my first major investment was Infosys."

From the Book Masterclass with Super Investors; no stock reco

items.title: RT Ancient Hypotheses: 🚨 Breaking News: Mohenjo‑daro is far older than previously believed. Archaeologists have just announced. This news feels le...
items.description_html:
RT Ancient Hypotheses
🚨 Breaking News: Mohenjo‑daro is far older than previously believed. Archaeologists have just announced.

This news feels less like a surprise and more like a confirmation.

From its indecipherable script to engineered water systems, Mohenjo‑daro has always behaved like a civilization with a longer history than the textbooks allowed.

A city like that, occupied from 2600–1900 BCE, doesn’t speak to a sudden emergence.

New dates pushing activity back toward 3300 BCE only confirm what the architecture has been telling us.

Things just keep getting older.

items.title: Unless you build, you don't learn. If you are using Codex or Claude Code or even Git agents, always have two files: 1. Architecture md: describes ever...
items.description_html:
Unless you build, you don't learn. If you are using Codex or Claude Code or even Git agents, always have two files:
1. Architecture md: describes every architecture decision, what to build & the sequence of build. If you close VSCode & there is no session memory, this file is your savior.
2. Decisions md: make sure your codegen write every decision it made into a decisions markdown. Ask it to also record why it selected something over alternative. This gives you way more learning than anything else.
Ensure you keep updating architecture md so that you don't need a separate State md file.

items.title: FY26 was a year of broad-based recovery turning into selective outperformance. The sector ended March with every segment in green —> PVs +8.4%, 2Ws +...
items.description_html:
FY26 was a year of broad-based recovery turning into selective outperformance. The sector ended March with every segment in green —> PVs +8.4%, 2Ws +12.5%, CVs +12.5%, Tractors +18.5% (retail).

Growth is great but the real thing in OEM has been the shift in MS which one should look for -

> In terms of shift in MS :- Winners: M&M (PV+Tractor), TVS (2W+EV+3W+Export), Tata (PV+CV), Ather (E-2W); Losers: Maruti (PV share), Hyundai (PV volumes), Ola Electric (E-2W), Honda 2W (strategic drift)

> E-2W at 9.8% (Mar26) :- expect double-digit in FY27. E-PV at 5% — this is where E-2W was 18 months ago. The ancillary supply chain implications: motor controllers, BMS, wiring harnesses, thermal management all might see volume inflection.

> CNG is 23.6% of PV retail in Mar26. This benefits :- gas distribution companies (IGL, MGL, Gujarat Gas), CNG kit suppliers, and dual-fuel engine component makers. The CNG-to-EV transition in PVs is a 5-7 year bridge

> Also interestingly inventory cycle might be your friend now as post this fast growth period the dealer inventory is at 28 days now which is low. No channel correction risk in the near term. Watch for any build-up above 40 days

> Crude oil and input cost volatility from Middle East tensions could compress OEM margins. Planned price hikes by OEMs in Q1FY27 could slow demand if consumer sentiment weakens.

Disclaimer - Not a recommendation to buy and sell

I wrote this at the beginning of the war. I don't like to predict things, but it appears that the initial probabilities I'd spoken of have kinda sorta come about.

The war will continue, but now there is an attempt to get boots on the ground? Whatever happens, don't underestimate Iran's capabilities in a conventional war. They've planned for this a long time.

What will matter now is how commerce flows. A month is already eating up the buffers built of - of oil, of anti-missile defenses, of other things. The world needs a trade reset of sorts to diversify dependence.

items.description_html:
Q4 FY26 Business Updates: Indian Banks showing solid momentum!

Several banks have rolled out strong provisional updates for the quarter ended Mar’26:

HDFC Bank: Advances up 12% and Deposit up 14.4%. Q4 & FY26 audited results expected on April 18 (board meet confirmed)

Punjab National Bank (PNB): Global business +10.8% YoY to ₹29.72 lakh Cr | Deposits +9.25% | Advances +13%

Bank of India: Global business +14.5% YoY to ₹16.98 lakh Cr | Deposits +13.6% | Advances +15.7% (RAM +18.6%)

Indian Bank: Total business +13.1% to ₹14.98 lakh Cr | Deposits +12.6% | Advances +13.6%

Central Bank of India: Total business +15.7% to ₹8.13 lakh Cr | Advances +18.9% | Deposits +13.4%

CSB Bank: Gross advances +27% to ₹40,364 Cr | Deposits +20% to ₹44,246 Cr (CASA slipped marginally)

South Indian Bank, Tamilnad Mercantile Bank & Karnataka Bank: Advances up 15-20%+, deposits growing steadily with improving CASA mix in most cases

Credit demand stays healthy, deposit mobilisation continues, and balance sheets are expanding robustly.

Classic signs of a resilient banking sector!

items.title: RT Pranay Kotasthane: Details of India's semiconductor manufacturing projects are scattered across hundreds of press releases, news reports, and gover...
items.description_html:
RT Pranay Kotasthane
Details of India's semiconductor manufacturing projects are scattered across hundreds of press releases, news reports, and government announcements. There's also a lot of hype and misreporting. So I built a tracker using Claude Code to fix this. It scores each project on technical complexity (benchmarked against a 3nm fab), tracks slippage from original announced dates, and links every data point to its source.

It's open source. If you have a status update, a correction, or know about a new facility, there's a form to contribute. Let's build this together.

items.title: Self-awareness is the key to clear thinking. It helps you spot when emotions, ego, or pressure are making choices for you instead of reason. Without i...
items.description_html:
Self-awareness is the key to clear thinking. It helps you spot when emotions, ego, or pressure are making choices for you instead of reason.
Without it, you react automatically, make small mistakes, and miss the bigger picture. Noticing your impulses, your "internal mad person", lets you pause, calm down, and respond thoughtfully.

Pulak Prasad says to be very lazy, and Munger reminds us never to interrupt compounding.
Neuland tested my patience so much that I turned into a crocodile, thick-skinned to volatility and deep drawdowns.
Ultimately, it’s all about self-awareness and discipline. Always validate people first; never play the long-term games with short-term folks!


Antifragile Thinking: An anti-fragile investor woke up after 4 years and 2 months and wanted to check the returns.

Investor: Alexa, what's my compounded return in #LaurusLabs since December 2019?
Alexa: "It's 55% excluding dividends."

Investor: Wow, that's great! I can go back to sleep now for the

items.description_html:
As expected, Passenger Vehicle (PV) Sales in India for FY26 come in strong

Sales of PV in India were at 4.7 mn in FY26, up 8.3% v/s FY25 (when sales were 4.34 mn units)

Factors - fuelled by improved affordability among consumers post the GST cuts by the Centre.

Senior auto industry executives said that while there has been no major disruption in demand, supply, and production yet due to the conflict in West Asia, they are closely monitoring geopolitical developments.

Maruti - The company closed the year with passenger vehicle sales of 1.82 million units vs 1.76 million units in FY25.

The company had pending bookings for 190,000 vehicles and a network stock of 12 days at the close of the fiscal year.

Exports last fiscal year surged 34% to 0.45 million units.

Src - ET | no reco

items.title: Everyone is tracking Dhurandhar 2’s box office ticket sales, but the real story will unfold somewhere else - Of course, thanks to the amazing respons...
items.description_html:
Everyone is tracking Dhurandhar 2’s box office ticket sales, but the real story will unfold somewhere else -

Of course, thanks to the amazing response, the quarter will be good for Multiplex operators for PVR Inox and others

However, the real story for PVR Inox will unfold in the Food & Beverages (F&B) segment and Ad Revenue.

You’re locking a consumer inside the theatre for 4.5–5 hours. F&B spend per head goes up (multiple orders, not one), and F&B carries 60–70% gross margins

Let’s take a live example: I went to watch the movie and paid Rs 1,400 for two recliner tickets. I saw that a popcorn + Cold drink combo was almost 1000 Rs, and even if you buy some other items between 2 people, you can easily spend Rs 1000

It's like spending almost 60-70% of the total amount spent on tickets on Food and beverages

PVR Inox is not a multiplex company, it is a Food and beverage company 😊

items.title: RT Vasant Shetty | Building Mundhe Banni: Would you trust an AI with your money? Would you pick a stock just because an AI suggested it? Today, your I...
items.description_html:
RT Vasant Shetty | Building Mundhe Banni
Would you trust an AI with your money? Would you pick a stock just because an AI suggested it?

Today, your Instagram and YouTube feeds are full of voices claiming that AI can pick better stocks than any human ever could. That it can help you make money effortlessly. It sounds convincing. It feels logical.

But when we asked @deepakshenoy this on the @mundhebanni podcast , his answer was very different.

He pointed out something subtle but dangerous. AI can be nudged, guided, even manipulated until it finally agrees with you. Ask the same question in slightly different ways, push it enough times, and it will eventually say, “Yes, this is a good company.” Not because it truly believes it, but because it is designed to respond, not resist.

And here’s the catch. Most people don’t realise how much their own bias shapes the questions they ask. If your question carries a bias, the AI often ends up confirming it. That’s where things start going wrong.

He compares this to that one doctor we have all seen. No matter what the problem is, fever, fracture, anything, the answer is the same. Take Calpol. AI can feel exactly like that. Standard answers. Safe advice. Generic solutions that sound right, but completely miss the nuance of your actual situation.

But investing is not generic. Your money is real. Your situation is personal. And the moment things get slightly complex, taxes, edge cases, unusual scenarios, AI starts guessing.

And if that guess goes wrong, there is no accountability.

That is why, at the end of the day, you still need human judgment while making financial decisions.

This is just one of many sharp insights from the episode. Easily one of the most practical conversations on financial literacy.

Full episode is now live, with English subtitles as well. Sort your good friday :)
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IMlMUIZ5VTg&t=27s

#DeepakShenoy #Podcast #Capitalmind #Kannada

items.title: India has seen a sharp decline in the incidence of malaria, sharper than in the rest of the world. In 1990, India recorded around 4,000 new cases for ...
items.description_html:
India has seen a sharp decline in the incidence of malaria, sharper than in the rest of the world.

In 1990, India recorded around 4,000 new cases for every 100,000 people, mirroring the global malaria incidence rate. By 2020, while the global incidence rate fell to 3,000 new cases for every 100,000 people, India’s rate dropped to less than 230 per 100,000 people.

These figures are based on modelled estimates from the Institute for Health Metrics and Evaluation, as data on directly reported cases of malaria from the Indian government remains limited and does not include state-level estimates.

However, malaria is not evenly distributed across India, with 80% of the disease burden concentrated among 20% of the population classified as "high risk".

In 2020, incidence rates were highest in Mizoram and Chhattisgarh (around 1 in 50 people affected), followed by Jharkhand (around 1 in 100 people affected).

Regions with high tribal populations are disproportionately affected, contributing to 32% of cases and 42% of malaria-related deaths, despite accounting for just 6.6% of India’s population. Malaria in these areas is driven by unique challenges, including difficult-to-reach forested and hilly terrains, climatic conditions favouring mosquito and parasite proliferation, and limited healthcare access.

What do malaria death rates look like across different states in India? Read @nileena_suresh's piece to find out: https://www.dataforindia.com/malaria-in-india/?utm_source=twitter&utm_medium=post

#Malaria #Health #PublicHealth #India #DataForIndia

items.title: RT Ankur Bisen: In July’24, Haryana govt notification allowed stilt + 4 floors buildings even on unapproved plans. The notification was challenged in...
items.description_html:
RT Ankur Bisen
In July’24, Haryana govt notification allowed stilt + 4 floors buildings even on unapproved plans. The notification was challenged in the high court that ordered a physical inspection and stayed the notification. In one of the poshest sectors in gurgoan, where houses sell north of half a million dollars, upon verification as part of this case, it was found that the 12 metres road had shrunk to 4 metres and the colony lacked infra for sewage, sanitation, garbage disposal and blocked aquifers. In this colony, the notification wanted more to be built without any audit of infra capacity.

This is a text book example of the less talked about aspect of India’s sanitation challenge i.e town planning is wired to design cities for a filthy outcome. It does so to exploit the only monetisation tool they have to sustain- stamp duties.

India has stepped into FY27 amid an extremely challenging global landscape marked by escalating geopolitical hostilities. The ongoing conflict in West Asia has resulted in a drastic jump in international energy prices and triggered supply-side constraints. Given the prolonged nature of the conflict and rising risks of disruptions, our projections for the Indian economy have been revised from our previous assessment following the outbreak of the conflict.

items.title: RT Capitalmind: After the 2023 debt tax change, pure debt funds lost their edge for anyone in the higher brackets. But you still need non-equity expos...
items.description_html:
RT Capitalmind
After the 2023 debt tax change, pure debt funds lost their edge for anyone in the higher brackets. But you still need non-equity exposure. So where do you go?

Hybrid funds. Four categories, all taxed like equity, all very different under the hood.

The net equity range runs from ~25% to ~75%.
When we traced the max drawdowns across each category from 2017-2025, it ranged from single digits (Equity Savings) to ~29% (Aggressive Hybrid), compared to 35-40% for pure equity.
The debt allocation becomes a real cushion.

Full breakdown on our blog: https://www.capitalmind.in/insights/the-buffer-unpacked-four-hybrid-categories-and-what-they-do

items.title: Every year, manufacturers such as Intel, TSMC, and Samsung produce billions of chips, each of which consists of billions of switches. These are semico...
items.description_html:
Every year, manufacturers such as Intel, TSMC, and Samsung produce billions of chips, each of which consists of billions of switches.

These are semiconductors, miniature constructions built on a silicon disc or wafer. You wouldn’t give at silicon in its raw sand-like state a second glance.

However, once processed, it has the capacity to alter conductive properties, which means you can use it to turn electric currents on or off – in other words, to transform a ‘0’ into a ‘1’. Silicon Valley, the birthplace of the chip industry, owes its name and fame to this unique material

Book SRC – Focus, The ASML Way

items.description_html:
Nestle India has a current Maggi production capacity of 14.16 Cr kgs a year

They are adding 2 cr kgs capacity

Standard Maggi packet weight (single piece) is 70g; Common packs are around 300 grams (4 pieces)

That means 202.3 crore single servings of Maggi can be sold from Nestle India Facilities (with 80% capacity utilization, so it's 162 crore servings)

Every day, 44.3 lakh servings are sold, every hour 1.85 lakh servings, every minute 3080 servings, and every second 50 servings

This is Just Maggi - Imagine the stats for Sting, Coke, Pepsi, Junk Food, and other Processed/ultra-processed foods

This makes me more bullish on the Indian Pharma ecosystem for sure :)

no reco!

items.title: RT Sandeep Kulkarni: I continue to believe a lot of pain is already factored in by the markets and near-term downside looks quite limited to me. Unles...
items.description_html:
RT Sandeep Kulkarni
I continue to believe a lot of pain is already factored in by the markets and near-term downside looks quite limited to me. Unless this stalemate drags on for another 3-4 months or if there is an equivalent of Pearl harbor/Hiroshima-Nagasaki it is unlikely that we will see a significant down move from this level.

Every big buying opportunity has this extreme uncertainty about a particular event. In 2008 it was about domino effect of Lehman collapse, in 2020 it was about effects of the lockdown and how long it would last, in 2022 it was a threat of a nuclear attack by Russia and an imminent recession sur o rate hikes/QT. Today there is fear about blowing up power plants, desalination plants, steel plants, etc.

In such periods, I like to fall back on the teachings of my guru Howard Marks. Check out this excerpt about investing sacred with intellectual humility. RT if it makes sense to you in these times.


Sandeep Kulkarni: In Mar 2020 markets went down first, markets bottomed out on the first day of the lockdown. Covid cases kept rising, lockdown kept getting extended, but the market kept going higher.
In 2022, after the Russia-Ukraine war, the market corrected sharply as commodity prices rose,

items.title: RT Richard Turrin: The IMF has a MUST-READ report covering Southeast Asia’s (SEA’s) QR-based digital payment revolution that is transforming cross-b...
items.description_html:
RT Richard Turrin
The IMF has a MUST-READ report covering Southeast Asia’s (SEA’s) QR-based digital payment revolution that is transforming cross-border payments, promoting local currency usage, and boosting trade and inclusion.

Humble QR payments, born in China back in 2014, are now changing all of SEA and especially Thailand, a local leader in digital payments, including CBDC.

The IMF reports domestic QR payments are exploding throughout the region: 300% growth in Thailand, 550% in Malaysia, and 467% growth in the Philippines, all for 2023-24.

But what is happening now is even bolder as QR payments have just begun to go cross-border to reduce cost and time on Asia’s astounding 32% global share of cross-border transactions.

To do this, Asia is connecting QR payment systems to allow bilateral real-time transfers in a collaborative effort between governments, who built the real-time payment networks, and local private payment providers.

The drive for these connections is driven by tourism, with intra-ASEAN tourism accounting for 42% of all visits, and the region’s financial backbone, SME businesses.

SMEs benefit from cross-border QR payments in two ways:

↳ Expansion of their market reach into new countries.

↳ Establishment of a digital payment footprint that can be used to assess creditworthiness and bring greater inclusion.

Another key point is that these cross-border transactions use local currency and avoid the risk of using US dollars and currency shocks.

Read on for more QR correlations and to download the report......

items.title: Road accidents are a leading cause of mortality in India. 29 out of every 1,000 deaths in India in 2022 were attributed to road accidents. India repor...
items.description_html:
Road accidents are a leading cause of mortality in India. 29 out of every 1,000 deaths in India in 2022 were attributed to road accidents.

India reports the highest number of road accident deaths in the world, and accounts for 11% of the world's road accidents.

The impact is particularly severe on young men aged 15-29. Road accidents are the single largest cause of death among young Indian men, responsible for nearly one in four deaths.

Two-wheeler riders account for the highest number of fatality victims in road accidents in India – nearly half of all deaths in road accidents are of people on two-wheelers. According to police statistics, two-wheelers are also the leading cause of road accident fatalities, followed by cars and trucks.

The lack of usage of helmets is a serious concern when it comes to fatalities from two-wheeler accidents. In about 50,000 of the 75,000 fatalities involving two-wheelers in India in 2022, the victims were not wearing helmets.

Which states bear the highest burden of road accident deaths, and how do these patterns differ across India? Read @nileena_suresh's analysis to find out: https://www.dataforindia.com/road-accident-deaths/?utm_source=twitter&utm_medium=post

#Death #Accident #Roads #India #DataForIndia

items.title: In my experience, this is probably the first time the Mid & Small cap indices have outperformed the Nifty 50 during a crisis time. It's also extremely...
items.description_html: In my experience, this is probably the first time the Mid & Small cap indices have outperformed the Nifty 50 during a crisis time. It's also extremely rare to see the Mid & Small cap indices (avg of the two) beating the Nifty 50 near the bottom of the cycle. Their avg CAGR has outperformed during the last two tough years as well as all the below trailing years. I am not that surprised as their balance sheet, P&L and cash flow quality has substantially improved post COVID. I believe the biggest multi-baggers will emerge from this space as the earnings growth differential will be significant going forward. Because of the quality differential today vs during pre-COVID period, I would be surprised if their valuations correct to levels seen during the previous crashes. We probably have the best corporate quality and least stressed financials currently compared to any other previous big crisis.

items.title: RT Gaurab Chakrabarti: The U.S. is running Elon's playbook on LNG: Build it before the world knows it needs it. A decade ago, American LNG exports wer...
items.description_html:
RT Gaurab Chakrabarti
The U.S. is running Elon's playbook on LNG: Build it before the world knows it needs it. A decade ago, American LNG exports were near zero. Today the US is the world's largest exporter at 15 billion cubic feet per day with eight new terminals under construction. Capacity doubles to 29 billion cubic feet per day by 2029. Total capital committed: $150 billion. The contracts are 20-year take-or-pay, denominated in USD. During COVID, buyers cancelled every cargo they could. They still paid billions in fixed fees. Then Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz. Qatar, 20% of global LNG supply, went offline. Vessel transits dropped from 129 per day to 4. Europe needed a replacement. BUT the contracts were already signed. US share of European LNG: 27% in 2021, 56% today. Germany went from zero LNG imports to sourcing 94% from America. Japan is locked through 2050. American LNG is a 20-year legal obligation with no exit clause.

items.title: Economic activity is not uniformly spread across the country. Among India’s 28 states and eight union territories, there are significant differences ...
items.description_html:
Economic activity is not uniformly spread across the country. Among India’s 28 states and eight union territories, there are significant differences in relative affluence as well as in the trajectories of their economic development.

India’s economic output is concentrated in the southern and western states. They are home to a third of India’s population but contribute more than half of its economic output.

Over the last four decades, India’s southern and eastern states have seen a considerable divergence in their contributions to the national economy, despite both regions having comparable populations. Southern states like Karnataka now contribute relatively more, while eastern states like West Bengal have seen their share decline.

This divergence becomes even sharper in terms of per capita income, or state domestic product relative to population, since the richer states are also the ones where population growth has slowed much more dramatically.

In 1981, West Bengal’s per capita income was 16% higher than Karnataka’s. By 2021, Karnataka’s per capita income was more than double West Bengal’s, showing a reversal in their positions and a widening gap between one of India’s richest and one of its poorest states.

What is the share of different sectors in the economic growth of states across India? Read @akwaghmare’s piece to learn more: https://www.dataforindia.com/state-economies/?utm_source=twitter&utm_medium=post

#Economy #States #GDP #India #DataForIndia

Register Now: https://lnkd.in/dWk_Buy9

CareEdge Ratings is hosting a webinar on Commercial Vehicle Financing: Emerging Trends and Future Prospects, aimed at providing a nuanced and data-driven perspective on the evolving CV financing landscape.

📅 March 30, 2026
⏰ 4:00 – 5:00 PM

After navigating a prolonged phase of subdued growth due to the pandemic and regulatory transitions, the CV financing sector recorded a strong AUM expansion in FY24. While growth moderated in FY25 amid stagnant CV sales, momentum has re-emerged in H2 FY26, supported by improving economic activity and the implementation of GST 2.0. However, global geopolitical developments continue to pose potential risks, underscoring the need for close monitoring of sectoral fundamentals.

In this session, CareEdge Ratings’ experts will analyse recent trends, evaluate performance indicators across CV-focused NBFCs, and discuss the key drivers and risks shaping the sector’s medium-term outlook.

Key discussion points include:

▶️ Growth trajectory of the CV financing segment
▶️ Performance metrics and operating trends of CV-focused NBFCs
▶️ Asset quality trends and risk assessment
▶️ Sector outlook, including key drivers and emerging risks

Join us for a structured and insightful discussion on the future direction of commercial vehicle financing.

#CareEdgeRatings #Webinar #CommercialVehicleFinancing #NBFCs #FinancialServices #IndustryOutlook #AssetQuality

items.title: Closing FY2026 - 9 Months ago I took the leap of faith and went back into startup mode and Here’s how it’s going - Got myself SEBI Registered in 202...
items.description_html:
Closing FY2026 - 9 Months ago I took the leap of faith and went back into startup mode and Here’s how it’s going

- Got myself SEBI Registered in 2025
- Grew Socials from an average of 320k subs to almost 450k in 9months on Insta and YT
- Distribution across platforms grew to 1Mn+
- Started with a team of 6, Today we are at 18 (3 remote)
- Launched Smallcases on October 2nd 2025, Beat our yearly target of 1,000 subs in 1 month, revised it to 3,000 subs for October 2026, we are 80% there.
- Crossed 26 Cr in AUM, aiming to cross 50 Cr soon
- Launched 7BAR - Swing Trading Cohort, Crossed 3,300 Members in 9 months, our fastest growing vertical
- Both 7BAR and Smallcase operate at 80% Margins today, lean team, Good Value add
- Did 3 Offline events in Bangalore, Mumbai and Jaipur. Next is Hyderabad and Chennai - We don't make money on this as its a part of our Investor education initiative

Big Bets and Cash Burners
- Building a Fintech app in Stealth, Will be launching in the next 3-4months - Bootstrapped
- Will be along the lines of Stock Advisory and My distribution to start with
- Aim to get 7,000 Paid Users in Year 1 without Ad Spend (low estimates)
- Invested in 3 Shadow Channels on Social Media to support the App
- Team size should go up to 25 and our current office would sadly max out.

Investments
- Last year I Invested/ Incubated a Studio Company called Studio Ionique, goal was to grow my channel, service other customers and build many IP channels to help our products
- As of year closing (9months), The Biz closed at 1.06cr topline
- We have built 9 IP channels out of which 3 have crossed 50k subs (without my face) rest in incubation phase
- Social Media Services wing - We are currently working with 5 customers only, generating an average of 17L a month (3.5L average)
- Client breakup - 1 AI, 1 SAAS, 1 Global, 1 Youtube Biz, 1 Market Participant
- Year 2 should, at minimum close at 2.7cr topline for Studio

What's next
- We will be applying for PMS/AIF in 2027
- Converting from Individual RA to Corporate RA
- Due to having only an RA license, We are rejecting many customers who want us to manage their money/PF today
- Will have 1 more big launch this year by December 2026 that should generate 1cr+ revenue in 365days of launch with 50%+ margins
- Got in 2 co-founders, 1 who manages the entire Research side and the other who will build the tech stack

What I Need to Improve?
- Need to focus more on My Socials, kinda let it fall while building the biz from scratch. Realized the importance of having a co-founder haha
- Earlier we were a lean team, now we are scaling a bit too fast, hence have to start improving the hiring process.
- Earlier, we hired everyone on the metric of revenue realization - Now I need to hire people who won’t directly contribute to increasing revenue but more towards branding and comms
- Started focusing on health, Got inspired by my peers mainly @VaibhavSisinty , bro got ripped while juggling 100 things and he’s just 100ft away from my office. So no excuses for me now
- Mental Health kept going for a toss - Started up at the same time I became a dad. So There is a constant Guilt that I’m not spending a lot of time with my son during this stage, will fix that

Overall Both Businesses combined should be hitting 5cr+ topline next year while being very profitable, Full Steam Ahead🙌 Goal is to build a very good wealth management firm. Baby steps

Only company that never stops innovating.
When you have long conversations, a short term memory called KV Cache is used. As conversation get long, this becomes a bottleneck. That's why people were chasing memory stocks.
TourboQuant reduces the memory requirement by over 5-8 times!
That's why while chasing AI picks & shovels, if you are ignoring the model architecture changes, the tech changes in general, you will end up holding something that's at a risk of becoming not to valuable as it was earlier.

So for inference, we have 2 major breakthroughs thus far for inference:
1. Hard wired LLMs
2. TurboQuant
